requirement: PR #430 T-7 + R-2b: design, dead-window, and forward roles must be timestamp-disjoint; the dead window must be absent from every role and every derived artifact.

machine_checkable_assertions.assert: committed_epoch_ts_max < forward_epoch_floor (=> committed epoch contains ZERO forward-epoch bars => forward data must come from a NEW not-yet-accrued source)
dead window equals the consumed M1 holdout window (2026-03-01..2026-04-24) and lies strictly between design and forward, so R-2b exclusion is structural
design-M15 derived artifacts must satisfy per-file ts_max <= design_end (2026-02-28T23:59:59Z)
forward-epoch artifacts must satisfy per-file ts_min >= forward_epoch_floor (2026-04-25T00:00:00Z)

t1_feature_warmup_leakage_addressed.policy: Dead-window data is NEVER loaded for any purpose. All indicators/context features (incl. H1/H4) initialise ONLY from forward-epoch bars; the first W forward-epoch bars are an event-ineligible warm-up burn-in, W >= the longest feature lookback across all groups including H1/H4 context. Exact W frozen at implementation; verified at the gate-6 source-contamination audit.
t1_feature_warmup_leakage_addressed.consequence: No validation/holdout feature can read a dead-window price via warm-up, aggregation context, or spread tables.

overall: SOURCE_LEVEL_PROOF_PROVEN (A1-A4); BYTE_LEVEL_PROOF PENDING (A5 at implementation; A6 when forward data accrues)
